title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
description: The system is vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) attacks if it doesn't properly handle large or malicious inputs. An attacker could flood the system with requests or oversized data, exhausting server resources and rendering the application unavailable to legitimate users.
attack_vector: Sending large amounts of data to the system, triggering computationally expensive operations, or exploiting vulnerabilities that cause excessive resource consumption.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single IP address.
Use input validation and sanitization to prevent the system from processing malicious data.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter out malicious requests.
